---
title: Datalust Seq
description: Self-hosted structured log aggregation with real-time search and alerting.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `datalust/seq:2024.3`  
**Ports:** `5341` (ingestion), `8090` (UI)  
**Data:** `seq_data` (named volume)

## Introduction

[Seq](https://datalust.co/seq) is a self-hosted log server for structured (JSON) events. It provides real-time search, filtering, dashboards, and alerting across log streams from any application that writes JSON logs — including .NET (Serilog, NLog), Node.js, Python, and others.

The free tier supports a single user with no event retention limits.

## When to Use

- Your application already produces structured JSON logs
- You want to search and filter logs without log aggregation infrastructure (no Elasticsearch, no Logstash)
- You're developing .NET applications and use Serilog or NLog
- You want to set up alerts when specific log events occur

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph LR
    App1["Application 1"] -->|"HTTP POST :5341"| Seq
    App2["Application 2"] -->|"HTTP POST :5341"| Seq
    subgraph Seq["Seq Container"]
        Ingest[":5341 Ingestion API"]
        UI[":8090 Web UI"]
        Store["seq_data volume"]
    end
    Ingest --> Store
    Store --> UI
    Browser["Browser"] --> UI
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- No extra host requirements

## Ports

| Port | Protocol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `5341` | HTTP | Log ingestion — applications send events here |
| `8090` | HTTP | Web UI — search, dashboards, alerts |

Both ports are configurable via `SEQ_INGEST_PORT` and `SEQ_UI_PORT`.

## Volumes

| Volume | Contents |
|---|---|
| `seq_data` | Ingested log events, dashboards, signals, and admin configuration |

## Configuration

```bash
cd datalust-seq
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `SEQ_INGEST_PORT` | `5341` | Ingestion port |
| `SEQ_UI_PORT` | `8090` | Web UI port |
| `SEQ_FIRSTRUN_ADMINPASSWORDHASH` | _(empty)_ | Optional admin password hash. If unset, Seq starts unauthenticated |

**To set an admin password before first start:**
```bash
echo 'your-password' | docker run --rm -i datalust/seq config hash
# Copy the output hash into SEQ_FIRSTRUN_ADMINPASSWORDHASH in .env
```

:::caution[Unauthenticated by default]
If `SEQ_FIRSTRUN_ADMINPASSWORDHASH` is left unset, Seq starts with **no authentication** — anyone who can reach port `8090` can read and delete all logged events. Set this before exposing Seq beyond `localhost`.
:::

## Workflow

```bash
# Start
docker compose up -d

# View logs
docker compose logs -f

# Open UI
open http://localhost:8090

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove all data (events, dashboards, alerts config) — destructive
docker compose down -v
```

## Use Cases

**Application log collection (.NET/Serilog):**
```csharp
Log.Logger = new LoggerConfiguration()
    .WriteTo.Seq("http://localhost:5341")
    .CreateLogger();
```

**Application log collection (Node.js/pino):**
```bash
node app.js | pino-seq --serverUrl http://localhost:5341
```

**Real-time log tailing:**
Open the UI and use the query `@Level = 'Error'` to filter only errors.

**Alerting:**
In Seq UI → Signals → Create signal for a query pattern. Connect to a webhook for notifications.

## Integration

### Nginx Proxy Manager

Uncomment the [NPM opt-in block](/networking/#the-npm-opt-in-pattern) in `docker-compose.yml`, restart, and create a proxy host: `seq.yourdomain.com → seq:8090`.

### Portainer

Deploy as a Portainer stack. In the Environment variables UI, add `SEQ_INGEST_PORT`, `SEQ_UI_PORT`, and optionally `SEQ_FIRSTRUN_ADMINPASSWORDHASH`.
